payload.claim_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels participated in National Work Zone Awareness Week at events in Albany and Ardsley, highlighting the continued rollout of Automated Work Zone Speed Enforcement with theme 'Safe Actions Save Lives.'
payload.description: MTA Bridges and Tunnels recognized National Work Zone Awareness Week with statewide transportation agencies
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels

records.raw_text: As construction season begins, we recently stood with transportation agencies statewide in recognizing National Work Zone Awareness Week at events in Albany and Ardsley, which highlighted the continued rollout of Automated Work Zone Speed Enforcement and this year's theme, 'Safe Actions Save Lives.'

payload.claim_text: Enhanced signage visibility has contributed to lower collision rates.

payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels

records.raw_text: Enhanced signage visibility has contributed to lower collision rates

payload.claim_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels debuted an E-ZPass Customer Service Center pilot at the New York International Auto Show, resulting in improved customer understanding and more than 1,000 customer interactions.
payload.description: E-ZPass Customer Service Center pilot debuted at the New York International Auto Show
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels

records.raw_text: the debut of our E-ZPass Customer Service Center pilot. Our on-site presence resulted in improved customer understanding of E-ZPass, helped address concerns, and enabled more meaningful interactions with more than 1,000 customers.

records.display_name: Marine Parkway Bridge Gantry Lane Use Signals

payload.description: Improve safety and security at the Marine Parkway Bridge. Replacement of traffic lane control signals and upgrades to security cameras. New fiber-optic, programmable logic controller-based system for lane-use and bridge lift traffic control signals. New CCTV security cameras.
payload.partner_agency: MTA Bridges and Tunnels Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Division
payload.project_name: Marine Parkway Bridge Gantry Lane Use Signals

records.raw_text: A project to improve safety and security at the Marine Parkway Bridge, led jointly by MTA Construction & Development and MTA Bridges and Tunnels Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Division, is in its early stages. The $4.2 million contract, which involves replacement of traffic lane control signals and upgrades to security cameras, was awarded in December 2025 and is scheduled for completion in March of 2027.

records.display_name: Marine Parkway Bridge Lane Control Signal Replacement

payload.description: Replacement of existing lane-use and bridge lift traffic control signals with a new fiber-optic, programmable logic controller-based system. Enables distributed control and monitoring to each location.
